title: Sphaerolobium lineare
intro: Sphaerolobium lineare, also known as the Narrow-leafed Sphaerolobium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Sphaerolobium lineare include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Sphaerolobium lineare include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions such as skin infections, wounds, and respiratory issues.

It is a part of the genus Sphaerolobium, which consists of flowering plants native to Western Australia. Sphaerolobium lineare is further classified as a member of the subfamily Faboideae, one of the two main subfamilies within Fabaceae.
